name: Optimize web bridge initial session list load

description: Baseline harness for initial list_sessions RPC over 480 stored sessions

asi.hypothesis: Measure current startup list_sessions cost with a synthetic multi-workspace corpus before changing behavior.
asi.benchmark_shape: 8 workspaces x 60 sessions, 19 JSONL entries per session, median of 7 fresh adapter RPCs.
asi.initial_bottleneck_guess: list_sessions opens each JSONL through SessionManager.open and scans full entries for display metadata.

description: Read stored session list metadata without constructing SessionManager for every file

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding SessionManager.open for stored list rows will remove duplicate JSONL reads, full entry arrays, and index construction while preserving response shape.
asi.implementation_note: readStoredSessionMetadata parses each JSONL once for header, first user text, and latest session_info; live and cached session managers still use existing path.
asi.observed_result: Median list_sessions RPC improved from 29.46ms to 17.171ms on 480 sessions.

description: Stop scanning a session file after finding the first user display text

asi.hypothesis: Current display-name precedence returns the first user message before any explicit session_info name, so session files with a user message do not need a full scan.
asi.implementation_note: readStoredSessionMetadata now breaks as soon as it extracts first user text; sessions without user text still scan for latest session_info fallback.
asi.observed_result: Median dropped from 17.171ms to 8.437ms; benchmark corpus has an early first user entry, which matches common sessions.

description: Parse stored session metadata line by line instead of splitting whole files

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding full split arrays for every JSONL reduces allocation and lets the early first-user break pay off on startup scans.
asi.implementation_note: readStoredSessionMetadata now walks newline offsets over the read string and skips reparsing the header in the metadata loop.
asi.observed_result: Median improved from 8.437ms to 6.64ms with checks passing.

description: Precompute session-list recency sort keys during row construction

asi.hypothesis: Sorting hundreds of sessions should not repeatedly Date.parse the same timestamps in comparator calls.
asi.implementation_note: Session list rows carry a private activitySortValue and strip it before returning the RPC payload.
asi.observed_result: Median improved from 6.64ms to 5.841ms, a smaller but consistent allocation and Date.parse reduction.

description: Fast-path already canonical ISO timestamps in normalizeSessionTimestamp

asi.hypothesis: Most Pi session headers are already canonical toISOString values, so skipping Date reconstruction might reduce row build cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Median did not improve versus 5.841ms current best; regex check cost offset any Date normalization savings in this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: If timestamp normalization remains hot, consider computing normalized timestamp and sort value together without regex, but focus elsewhere first.

description: Use an array instead of Set while collecting stored session files

asi.hypothesis: Avoid Set construction for stored session paths and rely on append-time dedupe.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed because live-session directory duplicates were parsed before append-time dedupe could skip them.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying, check seenSessionPaths before readStoredSessionMetadata or keep the Set-based de-duplication.

description: Skip duplicate stored session paths before metadata reads without building a Set

asi.hypothesis: An array plus seenSessionPaths pre-read check might avoid Set construction while still skipping live-directory duplicates.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.841ms; iterating duplicates and checking the growing seen set was slower than Set de-duplication up front.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep storedSessionFiles as a Set unless file collection strategy changes more substantially.

description: Cache workspace metadata while building the session list

asi.hypothesis: Many sessions share the same workspace, so caching workspace display metadata might avoid repeated path.basename work.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed; Map lookup and cache maintenance cost more than recomputing the small metadata object for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add workspace metadata caching unless profiling shows path handling dominates on a much larger list.

description: Read only the head of each session file before falling back to full metadata scans

asi.hypothesis: Most display names come from the first user message near the top of the JSONL, so a bounded head read could avoid reading long transcripts.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed on the current startup corpus; manual open/read/close plus fallback logic outweighed savings for small and medium session files.
asi.next_action_hint: This may still help very large historical sessions. Revisit only with a benchmark corpus containing multi-MB JSONL files and reinitialize the workload metric.

description: Avoid trimming JSONL lines before JSON.parse during metadata scans

asi.hypothesis: JSON.parse accepts whitespace, so avoiding trim() on every scanned line may reduce allocation.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed slightly versus 5.841ms; the emptiness checks and raw CR handling did not beat trim() in practice.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave line trimming unchanged unless a profiler identifies it as hot under a different corpus.

description: Normalize session-list timestamps and sort keys with one Date.parse

asi.hypothesis: Session rows currently parse each timestamp once for response normalization and again for sort keys; combining both should reduce row construction cost without changing output.
asi.implementation_note: Added normalizeSessionListTimestamp for list_sessions rows while leaving the general normalizeSessionTimestamp helper unchanged for other callers.
asi.observed_result: Median improved from 5.841ms to 5.762ms; small but primary metric improved and checks passed.

description: Bypass SessionRuntime live-path check for non-live session running state

asi.hypothesis: list_sessions already branches on liveSessionFile, so non-live running checks can go directly to DetachedSessionRegistry and avoid repeated live-path lookups.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; the runtime helper was not a meaningful bottleneck and direct registry access did not improve the workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep using SessionRuntime.isSessionRunning for clarity unless larger profiling shows running-state lookup costs dominate.

description: Skip live-session directory readdir when it is already under the stored sessions root

asi.hypothesis: The stored session root scan already includes the live session directory in normal startup, so skipping the extra live-dir readdir could reduce duplicate path work.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed; path.relative checks and helper indirection cost more than the small duplicate directory scan in this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid path containment checks in the hot path unless the live-dir duplicate scan becomes much larger than one workspace directory.

description: Append session files into the root list without per-directory arrays

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding filter/map arrays and spread while enumerating session files might reduce startup allocations.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; the simpler filter/map implementation is faster under this directory shape.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep file enumeration as-is; the hot path is metadata parsing and sorting, not small per-directory arrays.

description: Return stored session metadata immediately when first user text is found

asi.hypothesis: The common path finds first user text early, so returning immediately might avoid a variable assignment and post-loop nullish coalescing.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; duplicate return-object construction did not improve the hot path.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the single post-loop return shape for readability and performance.

description: Store session-list sort keys on a symbol to avoid stripping rows before response

asi.hypothesis: Using a symbol sort key would be ignored by JSON serialization and could avoid the responseSessions map allocation.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; symbol property access and hidden-class changes outweighed removing the map.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep explicit responseSessions mapping with a plain activitySortValue field.

description: Hoist the session-list row comparator out of the RPC handler

asi.hypothesis: Avoid allocating a fresh sort comparator closure for every list_sessions RPC.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; closure allocation is not material and the hoisted helper did not help V8 optimize this path.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep comparator inline unless restructuring sort behavior for another reason.

description: Fast-path the first JSONL line as the session header before blank-line fallback

asi.hypothesis: Generated session files start with the session header, so parsing the first line directly might avoid the generic header scan loop.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; extra candidate trim and fallback branch cost more than the tiny generic loop.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not specialize header scanning unless removing trim or fallback safely becomes possible.

description: Cache live session running state once per list_sessions call

asi.hypothesis: Avoid calling ctx.isIdle() from inside row construction and make live running state a single per-RPC value.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; live running state is evaluated at most once in this workload, so the extra variable did not help.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not micro-optimize live running state lookup without profiling a case where live duplicates are appended repeatedly.

description: Avoid trim in list-specific timestamp parsing

asi.hypothesis: Stored session header timestamps are strings already, so Date.parse directly might avoid timestamp.trim() allocation in the list-specific normalizer.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; the prior helper shape was faster despite trim.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not alter timestamp parsing shape without a broader timestamp-format reason.

description: Shortcut display-name whitespace collapse when text has only single spaces

asi.hypothesis: First user messages often contain normal single spaces, so avoiding regex replacement in collapseWhitespace might reduce display-name extraction cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; extra regex test and helper call did not beat the existing replace/trim path.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave collapseWhitespace unchanged unless a broader text-normalization refactor is needed.

description: Inline the string-content fast path for stored session display text

asi.hypothesis: Most first user messages are plain string content, so avoiding the general extractMessageText helper call might reduce metadata extraction overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Median regressed versus 5.762ms; the branch/inlining shape was slower than the existing helper call.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep using extractMessageText for consistency unless optimizing with profiler-guided changes.

name: Optimize web bridge initial session list load with mixed long transcripts

description: Baseline mixed startup corpus with long historical session transcripts

asi.hypothesis: Broaden the workload to avoid overfitting to small JSONL files and capture the user's slow initial load when historical sessions are large.
asi.benchmark_shape: 8 workspaces x 60 sessions, 80 long sessions, 13.3MB total JSONL, median of 7 fresh adapter RPCs.
asi.baseline_note: Current best code from small/medium corpus reads full session files, so long transcripts increase median to 13.092ms.

description: Use bounded head reads for stored session metadata before falling back to full files

asi.hypothesis: For long transcripts, list_sessions only needs the header and first user message, so reading a bounded file head should avoid loading megabytes of unused JSONL data while preserving fallback for sessions without early user text.
asi.implementation_note: readStoredSessionMetadata reads 32KB, parses only complete JSONL lines, returns if it found first user text or the file fit in the buffer, and falls back to full-file parsing otherwise.
asi.observed_result: Mixed long-transcript median improved from 13.092ms to 9.651ms while checks passed.

description: Reduce the bounded metadata head read from 32KB to 8KB

asi.hypothesis: The needed header and first user entry are usually within the first few KB, so a smaller head buffer should reduce file I/O and string decoding for long sessions while still falling back safely.
asi.implementation_note: STORED_SESSION_METADATA_HEAD_BYTES changed from 32KB to 8KB; fallback remains unchanged if no complete early user metadata is found.
asi.observed_result: Mixed workload median improved from 9.651ms to 8.11ms.
